sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.42 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Is a free pro-$p$-group representable by matrices over an associative-com\-mu\-ta\-tive profinite ring with~1? A negative answer is equivalent to the fact that every linear pro-$p$-group satisfies a non-trivial pro-$p$-identity. This is known to be true in dimension$2$for$p 2$(A.\,N.\,Zubkov, Siberian Math.~J., 28, no.\,5 (1987), 742--747). It is also proved that a 2-dimensional linear pro-$2$-gro...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
